debug_info.goal:
α : Type
hα : OracleComp.SampleableType α
x : Bool
⊢ x ∈
    support
      (@OracleComp.HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect! (Vector Bool 2) Bool (OracleComp.hasUniformSelectVector Bool 1)
        #v[true, false])

failed_attempts:
simp only [uniformSelect!, Vector.get?, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, Pi.apply_def]
    cases x with
    | false =>
      -- For x = false, we use index 1
      use 1
      <;> simp
      <;> aesop
    | true =>
      -- For x = true, we use index 0
      use 0
      <;> simp
      <;> aesop

simp [support, @OracleCompHasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [Vector.get]) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try aesop)
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by
    cases x <;> cases y <;> simp [uniformSelectVector, selectElem]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [Vector.get]) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try aesop)
  probFailure_selectElem := by
    simp [uniformSelectVector, selectElem]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [Vector.get]) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try aesop)
₃

sop
  <;> trivial
support of `(xs[·]) <$> p` is the image of the support of `p` under `xs[·]`.

simp only [hasUniformSelectList, uniformSelect, OptionT.support, OptionT.run]
    <;>
    (try { contradiction }) <;>
    (try { cases x <;> simp }) <;>
    (try { aesop }) <;>
    (try { simp_all [Uniformize.distrib] }) <;>
    (try { simp_all [Uniformize.take] }) <;>
    (try { simp_all [Uniformize.head] }) <;>
    (try { simp_all [Uniformize.tail] }) <;>
    (try { aesop })

simp [support, evalDist]
    <;> fin_cases x <;> simp (config := {decide := true})

      <;> norm_num

so `x ∈ support (uniformSelect! #v[true, false])`.

   Alternatively, we can use that:
   - `support (uniformSelect! v)` is the set of elements of `v` because each element has non-zero probability.

    -- We know x is either true or false, and both are in the support of the distribution.
    -- Therefore, x is in the support of the selectElem.
    have h₁ : x ∈ support ($! #v[true, false]) := by
      simp only [support_eq_preimage, Set.mem_preimage, HasUniformSelect!.uniformSelect!, Pi.one_apply,
        Function.funext_iff, List.get_two, List.get_one, List.get_zero, Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero,
        Fin.val_one] at *
      fin_cases x <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})
    exact h₁
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by

    <;>

    (try
      {

    (try

        norm_num
        <;>
        simp_all (config := {decide := true})

  probFailure_selectElem := by

    -- Therefore, for any x : Bool, x is either true or false, and hence in the support.

show that it is in the support of the distribution.
    have h : x = true ∨ x = false := by
      cases x <;> simp
    cases h with
    | inl h =>
      -- Case x = true
      simp [h, support, ProbComp.mem_support_iff]
      <;> (try decide) <;> (try simp_all [ProbComp.support, HasUniformSelect_demoVector]) <;> (try decide)
    | inr h =>
      -- Case x = false
      simp [h, support, ProbComp.mem_support_iff]
      <;> (try decide) <;> (try simp_all [ProbComp.support, HasUniformSelect_demoVector]) <;> (try decide)
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by
    -- Show that the probability of selecting x is the same as the probability of selecting y.
    have h₁ : x = true ∨ x = false := by
      cases x <;> simp
    have h₂ : y = true ∨ y = false := by
      cases y <;> simp
    rcases h₁ with (rfl | rfl) <;> rcases h₂ with (rfl | rfl) <;>
      simp [ProbComp.pr]
    <;> rfl
  probFailure_selectElem := by
    -- Show that the probability of failure is 0.
    simp [ProbComp.pr]
    <;> rfl
